_meta.author: Claude (va-legislature-refine overnight autopilot)
_meta.note: VA House of Delegates batch, districts 2-6 (all Northern Virginia Democrats), state tier. Adele McClure (HD-2), Alfonso Lopez (HD-3), Charniele Herring (HD-4), Kirk McPike (HD-5), Rip Sullivan (HD-6). Evidence-first finalization from primary roll-call / sponsorship records (LIS vote-details, REPRO Rising scorecard PDFs, Giffords endorsements, choicetracker, official accomplishment pages, news). Every FALSE cites a source; cells with no public evidence left null (reset_unspecified). All five returned uniformly progressive records with NO cross-party deviation. McPike was seated Feb 18 2026 (special election) so has near-zero GA floor record — scored from local resolutions, campaign platform, and his documented co-chair role on the marriage-equality amendment campaign; many cells legitimately null.

profile.religion: Listed as Baptist on the Wikipedia infobox; no first-person faith statement located — treat as unconfirmed.
profile.background: VA House of Delegates, District 2 (Arlington/NoVA); sitting since Jan 10, 2024. B.S. Economics, VCU; former Exec. Director of the VA Legislative Black Caucus; prior eviction-prevention and election-administration roles. EMILY's List 2025 Gabrielle Giffords Rising Star Award recipient.

adele-mcclure.notes_set: VA Delegate HD-2 (Arlington/NoVA), sitting since Jan 2024. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Documented progressive record: sponsored/voted YES on the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (HJR1/SJR247) and contraception-access bills; voted NO on restricting public abortion funding (HB404); EMILY's List 2025 award + REPRO Rising endorsement. Co-patron + YES on HJ9 (repeal same-sex-marriage ban); campaign pledges to expand statewide LGBTQ+ protections. Public-education-funding advocate (opposes diverting funds to school choice). Reform-oriented on justice ('supportive, not punitive' responses). Strongly pro-gun-control: sponsored mandatory firearm-locking-device bill (HB2064/HB158) and the dating-partner-loophole bill (HB1869); Giffords/Brady/Moms Demand endorsed. No conservative deviations found. Score 0/100 — all scored cells documented contrary to the rubric; thinner dimensions (Christian liberty, federal overreach, immigration, much of elections) left null for lack of a named vote.

0.note: Sponsored + voted YES on the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (HJR1/SJR247); states 'a person's right to bodily autonomy is fundamental' — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Voted the full pro-abortion-access slate (contraception bills, abortion-travel shield, doctor protection); voted NO on HB404 restricting public abortion funding — opposite of abortion-abolition.

4.note: EMILY's List 2025 Rising Star Award recipient and REPRO Rising VA endorsee — has accepted abortion-industry backing.

0.note: Co-patron and YES vote on HJ9, the constitutional amendment repealing Virginia's same-sex-marriage ban — does not affirm marriage as one man and one woman.

1.note: Co-patron of the amendment establishing an affirmative right to same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution.

4.note: Campaign platform pledges to 'expand statewide protections' for LGBTQ+ Virginians — supports promotion of LGBTQ+ identity in public policy.

0.note: Advocates a 'fully-funded public education system'; opposes diverting funds from public schools (no support for universal school choice/ESAs).

1.note: Backs 'supportive, not punitive' responses and reduced jail time; sponsored HB1886 removing a court-fee condition on deferred dismissal — soft-on-crime orientation.

Giffords/Brady/Moms Demand endorsed; sponsored mandatory firearm-locking-device (HB2064/HB158) and the dating-partner-loophole gun bill (HB1869) — supports magazine/assault-weapon and safe-storage regulation.

profile.religion: Not publicly stated (received the Virginia Interfaith Center's Legislator of the Year in 2014, but his own faith/denomination is not disclosed in available sources).
profile.background: VA House of Delegates, District 3 (S. Arlington / N. Alexandria); in office continuously since 2012 (formerly HD-49). First Latino Democrat elected to the General Assembly; founder of the Virginia Latino Caucus; former House Democratic Whip. A.B. Vassar; J.D. Tulane. Father emigrated from Venezuela undocumented — drives his signature immigration focus.

alfonso-lopez.notes_set: VA Delegate HD-3 (Arlington/Alexandria), sitting since 2012; founder of the VA Latino Caucus; chairs Agriculture/Natural Resources. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Deep, uniformly progressive record across nearly every dimension. Sanctity: NARAL 100% lifetime, co-patron 2020 Reproductive Health Protection Act. Marriage: recorded YEA on HJ9 (2025) and HJ3 (2026) same-sex-marriage amendment; chief patron of VA Human Rights Act expansion + an LGBTQ advisory board. Schools: chief patron HB913 reversing the transgender-student model policies; opposes charter schools. Justice: bans on no-knock warrants/chokeholds, limited mandatory minimums, helped abolish the death penalty. Immigration (his signature): DREAM Act, driver-privilege cards (HB911), anti-ICE bills HB1440/HB1441; clearly pro-immigrant/sanctuary-aligned. Guns: chief patron of concealed-permit-competence (HB916) + firearm tax bills; backs universal background checks. NO conservative deviation. Score 0/100. His 'resist federal authority' work is ANTI-ICE (opposite valence) so federal-overreach cells are NOT credited — left null. Christian-liberty/CBDC/ESG/E-Verify/birthright/IVF have no public record — null.

0.note: Self-reports a 100% lifetime NARAL Virginia rating; co-patroned the 2020 Reproductive Health Protection Act rolling back abortion restrictions — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: States he will 'protect access to reproductive healthcare including abortion'; full pro-abortion-access voting record — opposite of abortion-abolition.

4.note: REPRO Rising Virginia supported/endorsed winner; 100% NARAL — has accepted abortion-rights-industry backing.

0.note: Recorded YEA on HJ9 (2025, House 58-35) and HJ3 (2026, House 67-31) — the amendment repealing the one-man-one-woman marriage definition.

1.note: Voted to establish an affirmative right to same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution.

2.note: Chief patron of HB913 (2026) reversing the Superintendent's transgender-student model policies; created an LGBTQ+ advisory board — rejects, rather than affirms, the biological-sex framing.

4.note: Chief patron of the VA Human Rights Act expansion (HB925); pushes to 'expand employment and housing protections for the LGBTQ communities' — supports promotion of LGBTQ+ identity in policy.

0.note: Self-reports having 'defeated charter school legislation'; opposes charter expansion (no support for universal school choice/ESAs).

1.note: Chief patron of HB913 reversing the Superintendent's model policies, which had included parental-notification requirements for gender transitions in schools.

2.note: HB913 reverses restrictions on gender-ideology school policy — contrary to opposing gender-ideology curricula in K-12.
Self-reports expanding no-excuse absentee voting and Sunday voting — opposite of single-day in-person voting with limited absentee.

3.note: Self-reports having 'made ballot drop boxes permanent' — opposite of opposing drop boxes/mass mail-in voting.

0.note: Championed bans on no-knock warrants, chokeholds, and police militarization, and AG authority to investigate police departments — constrains rather than backs law enforcement.

1.note: Helped lead death-penalty abolition; pushes to 'limit mandatory minimums' — soft-on-crime orientation.

4.note: Helped lead the 2021 abolition of Virginia's death penalty — contrary to affirming the magistrate's duty to bear the sword (Rom. 13:4).

1.note: Authored the Virginia DREAM Act and driver-privilege-card law for undocumented Virginians — opposite of mandatory deportation.

2.note: Chief patron of HB1440 (bar federal immigration enforcement in 'protected areas') and HB1441 (limit LE agreements with federal immigration authority) — sanctuary-aligned.

0.note: Chief patron of HB916 (2026) adding a demonstrated-competence requirement for concealed-handgun permits — opposite of permitless constitutional carry.

1.note: Supports universal background checks, closed the 'Charleston Loophole,' chief patron of firearm/ammunition tax bills; caucus passed the assault-weapons ban.

profile.religion: Listed simply as 'Christian' (Ballotpedia/VoteSmart); specific denomination not publicly stated.
profile.background: VA House of Delegates, District 4 (Alexandria + part of Fairfax); House Majority Leader; in the General Assembly since 2009 (formerly HD-46). First African-American woman elected to represent Northern Virginia in the GA. B.A. Economics, George Mason; J.D., Catholic University. Attorney.

charniele-herring.notes_set: VA Delegate HD-4 (Alexandria), House Majority Leader, sitting since 2009. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Chief-patron-level leadership on the two highest-salience culture-war items: CHIEF PATRON of HJ1, the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment, and floor leader who carried the same-sex-marriage and felon-voting amendments through the House. Planned Parenthood + NARAL endorsed (100% NARAL scorecard). Justice: chief co-patron of death-penalty abolition; author of the automatic record-expungement law; co-sponsor of marijuana legalization. Guns: Giffords-endorsed, led the House on the 2024 assault-weapons ban, defended the Capitol gun ban. NO conservative deviation. Score 0/100. Genuine evidence gaps left null: family/child sovereignty (no school-choice/parental/curriculum vote isolated), Christian liberty, federal overreach, immigration (no Herring-specific recorded vote located), and most economic/election ballot-mechanic cells.

0.note: Chief patron of HJ1, the constitutional amendment guaranteeing a 'fundamental right to reproductive freedom' (abortion) — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Lead sponsor of the abortion-rights amendment — opposite of abortion-abolition legislation.

4.note: Endorsed by Planned Parenthood Advocates of Virginia (2025) and NARAL Pro-Choice Virginia (100% NARAL scorecard) — has accepted abortion-industry backing.

0.note: As Majority Leader, led House Democrats in passing and voted for the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment — does not affirm marriage as one man and one woman.

1.note: Voted to repeal Virginia's constitutional same-sex-marriage ban and codify an affirmative right to marry.
Chief co-patron of death-penalty abolition; author of the automatic record-expungement law; co-sponsor of marijuana legalization — decarceration/leniency orientation.

4.note: Chief co-patron of Virginia's death-penalty abolition — contrary to affirming the magistrate's duty to bear the sword (Rom. 13:4).

1.note: Giffords-endorsed ('crucial role passing gun safety bills'); led the House on the 2024 assault-weapons ban and defended the Capitol gun ban — supports assault-weapon and magazine restrictions.

profile.religion: Episcopalian (Episcopal Church) — stated on his official General Assembly profile.
profile.background: VA House of Delegates, District 5 (City of Alexandria); won a Feb 10, 2026 special election, assumed office Feb 18, 2026 (replaced Elizabeth Bennett-Parker). Former Alexandria City Council member (2022-2026) and longtime chief of staff to U.S. Rep. Mark Takano. B.A. Southern Methodist University. Openly gay; the only sitting GA member in a same-sex marriage.

kirk-mcpike.notes_set: VA Delegate HD-5 (Alexandria); seated Feb 18, 2026 via special election, so he has a near-empty GA floor-vote record — the 2026 regular-session major votes (incl. HJ3) occurred before he took office. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Scored from his Alexandria City Council record, campaign platform, and a documented LEADERSHIP role: he is campaign co-chair of 'Virginians for Marriage Equality,' the campaign to pass the same-sex-marriage amendment (and is himself in a same-sex marriage). Authored Alexandria abortion-access resolutions and strongly supports the reproductive-freedom amendment. Immigration: opposes 287(g) ICE agreements, bars enforcement at schools/hospitals (sanctuary-aligned). NO conservative deviation. Score 0/100 on the documented cells; the MANY cells with no record yet (justice, guns, elections mechanics, economics, school policy) are honestly left null — to be hardened once he has a session of recorded votes.

0.note: Authored Alexandria resolutions to protect abortion access and 'led Alexandria's response to protect reproductive health' after Dobbs; strongly supports the reproductive-freedom amendment — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Backs permanent constitutional enshrinement of abortion rights — opposite of abortion-abolition.

0.note: Campaign co-chair of 'Virginians for Marriage Equality' to repeal Virginia's same-sex-marriage ban; committed to 'lead the charge' for marriage equality — does not affirm marriage as one man and one woman.

1.note: Leads the campaign to enshrine an affirmative right to same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution.

2.note: Pledges to 'stand up for the rights of LGBTQ+ residents, particularly members of the trans community' — affirms, rather than rejects, transgender identity policy.

4.note: Platform centers advancing LGBTQ+ rights in policy — supports promotion of LGBTQ+ identity in public policy.

1.note: Pledges to 'fight to defend the rights of immigrants' and names immigrants among groups he will protect from federal action — opposite of mandatory deportation.

2.note: Opposes 287(g) agreements with ICE and supports barring immigration enforcement at schools and hospitals — sanctuary-aligned.

profile.religion: Not publicly stated (no denomination disclosed on any source located; do not infer).
profile.background: VA House of Delegates, District 6 (parts of Arlington & Fairfax / McLean); House Democratic Caucus Chair (since 2020); in office since a 2014 special election (formerly HD-48). Author of the Virginia Clean Economy Act (2020). B.A. Amherst; J.D. University of Virginia. Attorney ~30 years.

rip-sullivan.notes_set: VA Delegate HD-6 (Arlington/Fairfax), House Democratic Caucus Chair, sitting since 2014. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Uniformly progressive caucus leader. Sanctity: REPRO Rising A+ (2025)/A (2024), co-patron of the HJ1 reproductive-freedom amendment, Planned Parenthood endorsed. Marriage: backed the marriage-equality amendment (own session recap), Equality Virginia endorsed, passed 'Jacob's Law' protecting same-sex-couple parental rights. Guns (signature): AUTHOR of Virginia's red-flag / Extreme Risk Protection Order law, co-patron of the HB217 (2026) assault-weapons + 15-round-magazine ban, Giffords-endorsed. NO conservative deviation. Score 0/100. Honest gaps left null: religion, family/child sovereignty, Christian liberty, federal overreach, immigration (no named individual votes), and economic/election ballot-mechanic specifics. NOTE: his individual roll-call 'yea' on HJ9 and HB217 is from caucus-bloc passage + patron lists (LIS line-item pages are JS-rendered); patronage and his own recap make these solid.

0.note: REPRO Rising A+ (2025); co-patron of HJ1, the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment; pledges to enshrine abortion rights in the Constitution — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Vows to 'fight to ensure abortion rights become part of the Virginia Constitution' — opposite of abortion-abolition.

4.note: Endorsed by Planned Parenthood and REPRO Rising Virginia — has accepted abortion-rights-industry backing.

0.note: His own session recap states he 'backed constitutional amendment recognizing same-sex marriage rights' — does not affirm marriage as one man and one woman.

1.note: Backed the amendment repealing Virginia's same-sex-marriage ban; Equality Virginia endorsed.

4.note: Lists 'protect and advance LGBTQ rights' as a standing priority; passed 'Jacob's Law' protecting same-sex-couple parental rights — supports promotion of LGBTQ+ identity in policy.

1.note: Author of Virginia's red-flag/ERPO law and co-patron of HB217 (2026), the assault-weapons + 15-round-magazine ban; Giffords-endorsed — directly supports red-flag laws, magazine limits, and an assault-weapons ban.
